LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE MAY-06-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ROBERT YEE'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION AND FAILURE TO STATE A CLAIM, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT'S "MOTION TO DISMISS FOR LACK OF SUBJECT MATTER JURISDICTION AND FAILURE TO STATE A CLAIM" IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR FOR NONCOMPLIANCE WITH CCP 430.41 AND/OR 435.5 (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-11-2024 Set For Trial On Feb-10-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction And Failure To State A Claim
Off Calendar May-06-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. NATALIA MINASSIAN, Esq. of Hatkoff & Minasssian (tel: 818-990-5180) for Plaintiff.
Defendant ROBERT YEE, in pro per present.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ROBERT YEE's Motion To Dismiss For Lack Of Subject Matter Jurisdiction And Failure To State A Claim.

Matter discussed. The Court notes the matter was not properly contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's "motion to dismiss for lack of subject matter jurisdiction and failure to state a claim" is ordered off calendar for noncompliance with CCP 430.41 and/or 435.5. Though it is largely incomprehensible and cites no California procedural law, the motion appears to be a demurrer and/or motion to strike. The new response date is June 3, 2024.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
